name: HuskiesOfficial
description: Design system for HuskiesOfficial — the world's largest Siberian Husky community. An Arch Essentials brand. Aesthetic: arctic expedition. Night sky, aurora, snow ridge, and a heritage badge emblem. Warm, rugged, outdoorsy — never corporate, never cutesy.

rules: Speak as a member of the pack, not as a company addressing customers.
Humor comes from shared husky experience (the screaming, the shedding, the escapes) — never from mocking the dogs or owners.
Be honest about the breed's difficulty. Trust is the moat; never oversell husky ownership.
Community first, monetization second. Never let a sales voice override the community voice.

aurora-teal.description: Primary accent. CTAs, highlights, key words.

aurora-violet.description: Secondary accent. Aurora bands, 'coming soon' states.

husky-amber.description: The amber eye. Pairs with husky-blue; use sparingly, always as the counterpart.

bone.description: From the logo's outline. Brand neutral — eyebrow text, rules, borders.

bone-dark.description: Bone darkened for text on light backgrounds.

teal-dark.description: Teal darkened for text on light backgrounds (contrast-safe).

signature-pair.description: Heterochromia. The blue eye and the amber eye. This is the ownable brand detail — when both appear together they must be visually equal partners. Never use amber alone as an accent.

rules: Default to dark mode. The brand lives on night (#0A1322).
Aurora-teal is the ONLY color used for primary CTAs. Do not introduce new accent colors.
Bone is the connective neutral — it ties the logo's outline into the UI. Use it for eyebrows, rules, and secondary borders.
Never place the dark-wordmark logo on a dark background, or the light-wordmark logo on a light background. See logo.usage.
Text on night must meet 4.5:1 contrast. ice = 14.3:1, snow-gray = 6.4:1, bone = 9.1:1. teal-dark and bone-dark exist for light backgrounds.

display.usage: Headlines, section titles, stats, buttons, logo wordmark. Its slightly-imperfect grotesque character matches the rugged brand tone.

body.usage: All body copy, labels, form fields.

rules: Gradient text (linear-gradient teal -> husky-blue -> violet, background-clip:text) is reserved for a single emphasized word in the H1. Never more than once per page.

elevation.card: none — use 1px border (line) on surface instead of shadow

aurora.description: The signature element. Blurred radial gradients (teal, violet, husky-blue) drifting behind the hero.

rules: All motion must respect prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. Disable aurora drift and reveals.

logo.concept: A heritage badge: a Siberian Husky head with heterochromatic eyes (blue + amber), flanked by the pack, under a mountain, above a pine treeline. Encircled by a bone ring.

badge.description: Circular emblem, no text. Transparent. Universal — works on any background.

mark.description: Small badge for nav headers and footers.

lockup-dark-bg.description: Badge + WHITE wordmark. Use ONLY on dark backgrounds. Contrast 13.1:1 on night.

lockup-light-bg.description: Badge + DARK wordmark. Use ONLY on light backgrounds. Contrast 15.7:1 on white.

avatar.description: 512px, night background. Circle-crop safe: 0px loss when cropped to a circle by Facebook/Instagram.

must: Choose the lockup by BACKGROUND, not by preference. Dark bg -> lockup-dark-bg. Light bg -> lockup-light-bg.
Maintain clear space equal to 8% of the logo's width on all sides.
For any circular crop (social avatars), use the avatar asset — it is padded to survive the crop with zero loss.
never: Never stretch or change its aspect ratio.
Never place the dark-wordmark lockup on a dark background (contrast fails at 2.1:1).
Never crop the badge tighter — the ears and chest ruff span the full circle and clipping them destroys breed recognition.
Never use the badge below 48px; use the favicon assets instead.

logo.format-note: Current assets are raster PNG. Vectorize before any print/merch run — do not upscale the PNG.

card-coming-soon.usage: Products not yet launched. Includes a 'Notify me' button that fires a GA4 + Pixel event for demand validation.

stat-strip.description: Social proof. Big display numbers with bone uppercase labels beneath.

patterns.hero: Aurora background + starfield -> badge logo -> eyebrow pill -> H1 (one gradient word) -> subhead -> email capture card. Single CTA above the fold.
patterns.email-capture: Always lead with the lead magnet (the free Husky Owner's Cheat Sheet), never with 'subscribe to our newsletter'. Include a hidden `source` field populated from ?utm_source= for channel attribution.
patterns.monetization-modules: Revenue sections (e.g. affiliate 'Gear the Pack Trusts') are built but hidden behind a body class flag (affiliate-off / affiliate-on). Build dormant, activate later — never rebuild.

accessibility.mobile-first: 80%+ of traffic arrives via the Facebook in-app browser on mobile. Test there first.
